events.message: Reproducible pattern across R1 and R2: whenever exactly 1 real (non-Tome-bonus) action remains in the Investigation phase, the action menu incorrectly drops all normal 1-action options (Move, Take resource, Play [cost] cards) and only offers Fast events (e.g. Mind over Matter, which costs 0 actions) plus Pass. R1: after Move to Guest Hall left '1 action left', menu omitted Move to Bedroom/Bathroom, Take resource, Play Flashlight (2 res, affordable), Play Scrying (1 res, affordable) - confirmed via a probe invalid-option call that these truly weren't legal choices at that decision point. R2: same thing at Bedroom after Investigate left '1 action left' - Move to Guest Hall, Take resource, Play Flashlight/Scrying/Working a Hunch all missing. In both cases the SAME actions were freely available one action-count earlier (2 actions left) and should still cost only 1 action to perform with 1 remaining. This looks like the last normal action is being conflated with Daisy's separate Tome-only bonus action slot (which legitimately restricts to Tome abilities only) - i.e. an off-by-one in how 'actions left' is computed/gated for the action menu. This is costing me one real action nearly every round.
